goal: Remove brittle seal instruction string-matching test from risk guidance branch

summary.title: Remove brittle seal instruction string-matching test
summary.what: Deleted the seal_instruction_test.go file that asserted exact-ish sealInstruction text fragments. The production seal --prepare risk guidance remains in internal/engine/seal.go.
summary.why: The test was too brittle and low-value: it coupled behavior to prompt wording instead of exercising a meaningful interface. Removing it keeps the branch focused on the guidance change without adding a string-matching regression test that would create maintenance noise.
summary.user_goal: 删除这个字符串匹配式测试，只保留收紧 risks 指令的实际代码改动。

decisions.chose: Remove the brittle wording test without replacing it.
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly rejected this style of string-matching test, and there is no better narrow behavioral test for static instruction copy without introducing a larger prompt/schema abstraction.

rejected.alternative: Keep the test as a prompt regression guard.
rejected.reason: It is overly coupled to literal wording and would fail on harmless copy edits.

rejected.alternative: Replace it with another prompt text test.
rejected.reason: That would preserve the same failure mode under a different shape.

architectural_claims: The risk guidance remains instruction-only production behavior without a dedicated string-matching unit test.
behavioral_changes: Removes the brittle sealInstruction string-fragment test from the branch.
